mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with month-over-month FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.5%, consumer spending power deteriorates sharply, causing corporate earnings revisions to turn negative and driving S&P 500 forward returns down over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of months where UNRATE exceeds 7.5%, or the signal out-of-sample accuracy drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales and the federal funds rate indicates a policy response to demand shocks, which stabilizes market expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS declines exceed 2% monthly while FEDFUNDS falls, with a Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, month-over-month increases in the industrial production index signal improving factory utilization and supply chain normalization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day S&P 500 returns during recovery phases, with a hit rate below 45% across all post-2001 recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue fundamentals, which mechanically drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns over any 5-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 500,000 units, construction sector contraction and negative wealth effects suppress consumer confidence, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 60% of months where HOUST stays below 500,000, invalidating the wealth effect transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and industrial production confirm broad-based economic stabilization, reducing recession risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and INDPRO uptrend fails to predict positive 20-day returns when both series rise simultaneously, with a predictive accuracy below 50% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-rate environment, accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces eventual monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rising CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative 60-day returns when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, with a Rank IC above 0.05 in rolling windows.
